message:
feat(server): robust HTTP supervisor lifecycle with timeout-escalated shutdown, watchdog thread, and retry respawn loop

Server (lib.rs):
- Add HTTP_SERVER_STOP_JOIN_TIMEOUT and HTTP_SERVER_FORCE_CLOSE_JOIN_TIMEOUT
  constants for two-phase graceful shutdown: drain first, then force-close
- Extract stop_http_server_instance_with_timeouts() and join_http_server_thread()
  for structured shutdown with timeout escalation via spawn_http_server_join_waiter
- Add respawn_http_server_instance_with_retry() generic retry loop with
  configurable abort predicate, error callback, and backoff — replaces
  inline retry logic in both crash-exit and liveness-probe restart paths
- Switch readiness handshake from unbounded channel to sync_channel(0) so
  a dropped receiver is detected immediately and the server thread can
  self-terminate cleanly
- Add supervisor_watchdog thread that monitors supervisor_exit_rx and
  triggers TUI shutdown if the supervisor exits unexpectedly
- Handle conn.Close() errors in WebSocket readPump/writePump and doctor
  check API

TUI poller (tui_poller.rs):
- Enhanced polling logic and event handling improvements

Robot CLI (robot.rs):
- Improved robot command output formatting and error handling

Core config and setup:
- config.rs: configuration refinements
- setup.rs: setup flow improvements

Storage (storage/lib.rs):
- Storage layer enhancements

Mail UI:
- mail_ui.rs: UI rendering improvements
- mail_unified_inbox.html: template updates

Tests:
- startup_compat.rs: updated compatibility test expectations

Beads:
- issues.jsonl: issue tracker state update

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -1153,7 +1153,7 @@
 {"id":"br-38wbs","title":"T3.2: Add glow and pulse effects for critical alerts and live indicators","description":"Critical system alerts and live status indicators should use subtle animation effects
to draw the operator's attention without being obnoxious.

GLOW EFFECT: Applied to critical alert text (e.g., "CRITICAL: 3 reservations expired")
- Glow oscillates brightness between base and +30% over 2-second cycle
- Only applied when severity >= CRITICAL

PULSE EFFECT: Applied to live status indicators
- "LIVE" indicator in status bar pulses gently
- "Recording" indicator when macro recording is active
- Health check "Probing..." text while check is in progress

IMPLEMENTATION:
```rust
use ftui_extras::text_effects::{TextEffect, Glow, Pulse};

let glow = Glow::new(palette.severity_critical, Duration::from_secs(2));
let alert_text = StyledText::new("CRITICAL: 3 reservations expired")
    .effect(TextEffect::Glow(glow));

let pulse = Pulse::new(palette.severity_ok, Duration::from_millis(1500));
let live_indicator = StyledText::new("LIVE")
    .effect(TextEffect::Pulse(pulse));
```

FILES: tui_chrome.rs (status bar), tui_screens/system_health.rs, tui_screens/reservations.rs","acceptance_criteria":"Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Critical alerts glow with 2-second cycle
- [ ] LIVE indicator pulses in status bar
- [ ] Macro recording indicator pulses
- [ ] Effects respect AM_TUI_EFFECTS env var
- [ ] CPU impact < 1% additional per animation

Plan-space hardening additions:
- [ ] Comprehensive unit tests cover happy-path behavior, edge cases, invariants, and failure modes for this bead scope
- [ ] Integration/E2E (or PTY/stress where applicable) scripts validate end-user flows, degraded-mode behavior, and recovery paths for this bead
- [ ] Test and benchmark outputs include detailed structured diagnostics (scenario IDs, timings, error/reason codes, artifact paths, and repro commands)","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilverHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-13T18:07:31.109806210Z","updated_at":"2026-02-15T17:52:27.161609632Z","closed_at":"2026-02-15T17:52:27.161579636Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["alerts","text-effects","tui"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-38wbs","depends_on_id":"br-hfcrr","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":700,"issue_id":"br-38wbs","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented text-effects updates in system_health.rs + reservations.rs (critical glow + probing pulse + expired reservation glow + tests). Remaining piece (tui_chrome LIVE/Recording pulse) is blocked by active reservation on tui_chrome.rs held by SilverFox. Validation is additionally blocked by external sibling dependency compile error in /data/projects/frankensearch/crates/frankensearch-core/src/time_travel.rs (reserved keyword 'gen' under Rust 2024).","created_at":"2026-02-15T05:56:12Z"},{"id":701,"issue_id":"br-38wbs","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented chrome-side LIVE/REC status pulse pipeline and wired recording state from MailAppModel. Added tests: status_segments_include_live_indicator and status_segments_recording_flag_controls_rec_indicator in tui_chrome.rs. Validation: rch still fails due missing sibling deps on remote workers; local cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib passes. Remaining validation blocker: lib tests fail in reserved file crates/mcp-agent-mail-server/src/mail_ui.rs (OverseerPayload missing Debug for expect_err) owned by AmberFalcon; requested handoff/unblock in Agent Mail thread coord-compile-unblock-mail-ui.","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:09:42Z"},{"id":702,"issue_id":"br-38wbs","author":"SilverHarbor","text":"Validation unblocked and complete. Confirmed glow/pulse behavior across reserved surfaces: status LIVE/REC pulse in tui_chrome + recording-state wiring in tui_app, critical/probing glow/pulse in system_health, and expired-critical glow summary in reservations. Verification: cargo check -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib; cargo test -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib status_segments_ -- --nocapture (11 passed); cargo clippy -p mcp-agent-mail-server --lib -- -D warnings; rustfmt --check on touched files. rch remote remained unstable due sibling path/dependency sync and worker disk-space issues; local fallback passed.","created_at":"2026-02-15T17:52:23Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-396j","title":"T5.6: Update scripts/am with note that port-reuse and token loading are now built-in","description":"## Objective
Finalize Track 5 migration by updating `scripts/am` messaging to native-first behavior for port reuse and token loading.

## Work
- Add explicit note that key startup behavior is now built into `am serve`.
- Provide clear pointers for compatibility usage and troubleshooting.
- Keep wrapper messaging aligned with governance deprecation policy.

## Deliverable
A compatibility wrapper that no longer appears authoritative over native serve behavior.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is self-contained: clear before/after command mapping, scope boundaries, rationale, and migration decision record.
- Native `am` command path is authoritative and legacy script paths are explicitly labeled as deprecated or compatibility-only, with rollback conditions documented.
- Operator-facing troubleshooting guidance includes where to find logs/artifacts and how to reproduce failures quickly.
- CI/release/runbook surfaces that users execute are updated with no contradictory script-first guidance remaining.
- Includes a verification checklist so future maintainers can re-audit correctness without external plan documents.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"AmberHarbor","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:25:09.665737407Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-13T06:09:03.994627090Z","closed_at":"2026-02-13T06:09:03.994606140Z","close_reason":"Completed: scripts/am now documents native-first startup behavior and compatibility scope","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-396j","depends_on_id":"br-17c93","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":703,"issue_id":"br-396j","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# T5.6: Update scripts/am with Built-In Feature Notice

## What to do
Update scripts/am to note that port-reuse detection and token loading are now
built into `am serve` natively. The script remains useful for dev binary discovery
(cargo run fallback) but is no longer the only way to get these features.

## Changes
1. Add note in the usage help text:
```bash
Note: Port-reuse detection and .env token loading are now built into
`mcp-agent-mail serve` natively. This wrapper script adds dev convenience
(cargo run fallback) but is optional for production use.
```

2. Add a comment at the top:
```bash
# NOTE: The port-reuse and token-loading features in this script are now
# built into the Rust binary (`am serve --reuse-running --env-file`).
# This wrapper is still useful for development (cargo run fallback).
```

## DO NOT add a deprecation warning
Unlike the other scripts, scripts/am is a dev convenience wrapper that provides
value beyond what the binary does (cargo run fallback, default settings). It's
not fully deprecated — just less necessary.

## Location
scripts/am (usage function + top comment)
","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:34:01Z"},{"id":704,"issue_id":"br-396j","author":"AmberHarbor","text":"Implemented native-first messaging update in scripts/am. Added top-of-file note that port-reuse and token-loading are now built into native startup (am start / mcp-agent-mail serve), and updated usage/help text with a Native-first section clarifying canonical entrypoint plus compatibility role of scripts/am. Also added examples for am and am start alongside script usage. Validation: bash -n scripts/am and bash scripts/am --help.","created_at":"2026-02-13T06:09:00Z"}]}
 {"id":"br-39eh","title":"Track 7: am share wizard — Native deployment wizard (replaces Python script fallback)","description":"## Purpose
Replace the remaining Python-based Share deployment wizard with a first-class native Rust implementation in `am`, so operator setup/deploy guidance is cross-platform, deterministic, and distributable with a single binary.

## Why this track exists
`am share wizard` currently shells out to `python` and resolves `scripts/share_to_github_pages.py` paths (`crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs:4022`, `crates/mcp-agent-mail-cli/src/lib.rs:4072`). This breaks portability and violates the “single compiled workflow” goal.

## Scope
- Native wizard flow for GitHub Pages / Cloudflare Pages / Netlify deployment setup.
- Typed plan model and JSON output for automation.
- Remove runtime dependency on Python wizard path resolution.
- Preserve (or improve) current operator guidance quality.

## Out of scope
- Re-architecting share bundle format itself.
- Removing static-export support.

## Design constraints
- No Python subprocess dependency in core wizard path.
- Keep behavior deterministic and testable under CI.
- Keep CLI UX parity with current `am` conventions (`--json`, actionable errors, exit codes).

## Deliverable
`am share wizard` is fully native, tested, documented, and independent of source-tree Python files.","acceptance_criteria":"## Acceptance Criteria
- `am share wizard` executes natively without requiring Python or source-tree script resolution.
- Interactive and non-interactive flows are both supported with deterministic behavior and stable `--json` schema.
- Dedicated e2e script suite (`T7.9`) validates realistic operator scenarios and failure paths with detailed transcript artifacts.
- Unit/integration coverage (`T7.7`) plus e2e evidence provide full parity proof against legacy behavior matrix (`T7.1`).
- Existing operator guidance quality is preserved or improved (clear prompts/actions/errors), and migration docs are updated.
- Legacy Python fallback dependency is removed or compatibility-gated only after test evidence is complete.","notes":"Track closure evidence: native share wizard path is authoritative in CLI; legacy Python fallback helpers/tests removed; docs updated to native guidance in EXISTING_MCP_AGENT_MAIL_STRUCTURE.md; native wizard E2E suite passes (tests/e2e/test_share_wizard.sh); CLI E2E case 24 updated to assert native non-interactive JSON validation error semantics. Residual unrelated risk noted separately: scripts/e2e_cli.sh Case 25 bind-collision expectation drift.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"track","created_at":"2026-02-12T01:43:54.956747808Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-02-12T22:00:59.213289662Z","closed_at":"2026-02-12T22:00:59.213262722Z","close_reason":"Completed: native share wizard cutover finalized and Python fallback dependency removed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["cli","python-migration","share"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"br-39eh","depends_on_id":"br-3ph9","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-03-05T05:24:05Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":705,"issue_id":"br-39eh","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"# Track 7 Deep Notes: Native Share Wizard

## Problem statement
Current wizard behavior depends on dynamic discovery of Python files and runtime Python availability. This is brittle in packaged deployments, CI images, and non-source-tree environments.

## Technical anchor points
- Current shell-out path is in CLI code (`run_share_wizard_in_cwd` and `run_python_script_in_cwd`).
- Failure UX currently points users to `python scripts/share_to_github_pages.py`, which is not reliable outside source checkout.

## Design intent
Move wizard logic to typed Rust modules with clear separation:
1. Environment/provider detection
2. Plan generation
3. Interactive renderer
4. Non-interactive JSON path

This separation keeps business logic testable and makes future provider additions low-risk.

## Guardrails
- Avoid hidden network calls in detection stage unless explicitly requested.
- Keep wizard outputs deterministic for same inputs.
- Retain high signal/noise ratio in operator prompts.

## Closure evidence expected
- Parity matrix artifact proving old/new behavior mapping.
- Integration tests covering interactive + non-interactive modes.
- Removal/gating of Python fallback path with migration note.

-{"id":"br-39gxa","title":"Align am robot navigate with canonical MCP resource semantics","description":"Fresh-eyes review found that am robot navigate does not preserve canonical resource semantics for several valid resource URIs. Affected cases include resource://projects query handling, resource://inbox/{agent} routing to actionable robot inbox instead of MCP inbox semantics, unsupported-but-valid resources like resource://views/urgent-unread/... and resource://mailbox-with-commits/..., and resource://file_reservations/{slug} bypassing resource-side cleanup and stale analysis. This creates CLI/MCP divergence for the same URI and should either be fixed in robot.rs or narrowed/documented explicitly.","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Codex","created_at":"2026-03-09T01:55:11.125222842Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-03-09T01:55:11.125222842Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
